<div class="quiz-shell edu-math-probability-checkpoint" data-default-total="4"></div>

<script>
  (() => {
    const DEFAULT_TOPIC = "sample-spaces-events";
    const BANKS = {
      "sample-spaces-events": [sampleSpaceQuestion, eventQuestion, complementQuestion, simpleProbabilityQuestion],
      counting: [factorialQuestion, productRuleQuestion, permutationCombinationQuestion, combinationQuestion],
      "conditional-probability": [conditionalMeaningQuestion, conditionalCountQuestion, independenceQuestion, bayesDirectionQuestion],
      "random-variables": [randomVariableQuestion, discreteContinuousQuestion, pmfValidityQuestion, expectedValueQuestion],
      "common-distributions": [bernoulliQuestion, binomialQuestion, uniformQuestion, normalQuestion],
      "expectation-variance": [weightedAverageQuestion, possibleOutcomeQuestion, varianceQuestion, fairGameQuestion],
      "probability-cumulative-review": [sampleSpaceQuestion, permutationCombinationQuestion, conditionalMeaningQuestion, pmfValidityQuestion, binomialQuestion, varianceQuestion],
    };

    document.querySelectorAll(".edu-math-probability-checkpoint:not([data-ready])").forEach(init);

    function init(root) {
      const config = readConfig(root);
      const state = { config, total: config.defaultTotal, index: 0, score: 0 };
      root.dataset.ready = "true";
      renderStart(root, state);
    }

    function readConfig(root) {
      const node = root.previousElementSibling;
      let parsed = {};
      if (node?.classList?.contains("edu-math-probability-checkpoint-config")) {
        try {
          parsed = JSON.parse(node.textContent || "{}");
        } catch {
          parsed = {};
        }
      }
      return {
        topic: parsed.topic || root.dataset.topic || DEFAULT_TOPIC,
        title: parsed.title || "Probability checkpoint",
        defaultTotal: Math.max(1, Math.min(20, Number(parsed.defaultTotal || 4))),
      };
    }

    function renderStart(root, state) {
      root.replaceChildren();
      const input = element("input", {
        class: "quiz-count",
        type: "number",
        min: "1",
        max: "20",
        value: String(state.total),
      });
      const button = element("button", { type: "button" }, "Start checkpoint");
      button.addEventListener("click", () => {
        state.total = Math.max(1, Math.min(20, Number(input.value) || 4));
        state.index = 0;
        state.score = 0;
        renderQuestion(root, state, makeQuestion(state.config.topic));
      });
      root.append(
        element("p", { class: "quiz-topic" }, state.config.title),
        element("div", { class: "quiz-start" }, element("label", {}, element("span", {}, "Number of questions"), input), button),
      );
    }

    function renderQuestion(root, state, question) {
      root.replaceChildren();
      let answered = false;
      const progress = element(
        "div",
        { class: "quiz-progress" },
        element("span", {}, `Question ${state.index + 1} of ${state.total}`),
        element("span", {}, `Score: ${state.score}/${state.total}`),
      );
      const feedback = element("p", { class: "quiz-feedback", "aria-live": "polite" });
      const next = element("button", { type: "button", disabled: "" }, "Next question");
      const options = radioList(question.options, (selected) => {
        if (answered) return;
        answered = true;
        const correct = selected === question.answer;
        if (correct) state.score += 1;
        feedback.className = correct ? "quiz-feedback correct" : "quiz-feedback wrong";
        feedback.replaceChildren(correct ? "Correct." : `Not quite. The answer is ${question.answer}.`, " ", question.explanation);
        options.querySelectorAll("input").forEach((input) => {
          input.disabled = true;
        });
        progress.lastElementChild.textContent = `Score: ${state.score}/${state.total}`;
        next.disabled = false;
      });
      next.addEventListener("click", () => {
        state.index += 1;
        if (state.index >= state.total) renderDone(root, state);
        else renderQuestion(root, state, makeQuestion(state.config.topic));
      });
      root.append(progress, stimulus(question.stimulus), element("p", { class: "quiz-prompt" }, question.prompt), feedback, options, next);
    }

    function renderDone(root, state) {
      root.replaceChildren();
      const restart = element("button", { type: "button" }, "Try again");
      restart.addEventListener("click", () => renderStart(root, state));
      root.append(element("h3", {}, "Checkpoint complete"), element("p", {}, `Final score: ${state.score}/${state.total}`), restart);
    }

    function makeQuestion(topic) {
      const bank = BANKS[topic] || BANKS[DEFAULT_TOPIC];
      return choice(bank)();
    }

    function sampleSpaceQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "What is the sample space for one coin flip?",
        stimulus: "one fair coin",
        answer: "{H, T}",
        distractors: ["{H}", "{T}", "{1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6}"],
        explanation: "The sample space lists all possible outcomes: heads and tails.",
      });
    }

    function eventQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "For a fair die, which event represents rolling an even number?",
        stimulus: "S = {1,2,3,4,5,6}",
        answer: "{2, 4, 6}",
        distractors: ["{1, 3, 5}", "{6}", "{2, 3, 5}"],
        explanation: "An event is a subset of outcomes that satisfy the condition.",
      });
    }

    function complementQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "What is the complement of rolling even on a fair die?",
        stimulus: "Even = {2,4,6}",
        answer: "{1, 3, 5}",
        distractors: ["{2, 4, 6}", "{1, 2, 3}", "empty set"],
        explanation: "The complement contains outcomes in the sample space that are not in the event.",
      });
    }

    function simpleProbabilityQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "What is P(rolling greater than 4) on a fair die?",
        stimulus: "favorable outcomes: {5,6}",
        answer: "2/6 = 1/3",
        distractors: ["1/6", "4/6", "1/2"],
        explanation: "There are 2 favorable outcomes out of 6 equally likely outcomes.",
      });
    }

    function factorialQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "Compute 4!.",
        stimulus: "factorial",
        answer: "24",
        distractors: ["8", "16", "4"],
        explanation: "4! = 4·3·2·1 = 24.",
      });
    }

    function productRuleQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "A meal has 3 mains and 2 drinks. How many meal choices?",
        stimulus: "choose one main and one drink",
        answer: "6",
        distractors: ["5", "3", "2"],
        explanation: "Use the product rule: 3·2 = 6.",
      });
    }

    function permutationCombinationQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "Which method fits choosing 3 students from 10 when order does not matter?",
        stimulus: "committee selection",
        answer: "combination",
        distractors: ["permutation", "factorial only", "conditional probability"],
        explanation: "Combinations count selections when order does not matter.",
      });
    }

    function combinationQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "Compute C(5,2).",
        stimulus: "choose 2 from 5",
        answer: "10",
        distractors: ["20", "5", "25"],
        explanation: "C(5,2)=5·4/(2·1)=10.",
      });
    }

    function conditionalMeaningQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "What does P(A | B) mean?",
        stimulus: "conditional notation",
        answer: "probability of A given B happened",
        distractors: ["probability of B given A happened", "A and B are impossible", "A or B"],
        explanation: "The condition after the vertical bar is the information already known.",
      });
    }

    function conditionalCountQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "A fair die is known to be greater than 3. What is P(even | greater than 3)?",
        stimulus: "restricted outcomes: {4,5,6}",
        answer: "2/3",
        distractors: ["1/2", "1/3", "3/6"],
        explanation: "Inside {4,5,6}, the even outcomes are {4,6}.",
      });
    }

    function independenceQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "If A and B are independent, what is P(A | B)?",
        stimulus: "B gives no information about A",
        answer: "P(A)",
        distractors: ["P(B)", "P(A and B)", "0"],
        explanation: "Independence means knowing B does not change the probability of A.",
      });
    }

    function bayesDirectionQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "Which probability reverses the condition in P(test positive | disease)?",
        stimulus: "Bayes direction",
        answer: "P(disease | test positive)",
        distractors: ["P(disease and test positive)", "P(test positive)", "P(not disease)"],
        explanation: "Bayes' rule helps reverse conditional probabilities.",
      });
    }

    function randomVariableQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "What does a random variable assign to outcomes?",
        stimulus: "random variable",
        answer: "numbers",
        distractors: ["only words", "only probabilities", "only graphs"],
        explanation: "A random variable is a numeric rule on outcomes.",
      });
    }

    function discreteContinuousQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "Which is continuous?",
        stimulus: "types of random variables",
        answer: "waiting time",
        distractors: ["number of heads in 3 flips", "die roll", "number of emails"],
        explanation: "Waiting time can vary over an interval, not just a list of separate values.",
      });
    }

    function pmfValidityQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "Is {0:0.3, 1:0.7} a valid PMF?",
        stimulus: "probabilities sum to 1",
        answer: "yes",
        distractors: ["no, sums to 0.7", "no, probabilities must be equal", "no, values must start at 1"],
        explanation: "The probabilities are nonnegative and 0.3+0.7=1.",
      });
    }

    function expectedValueQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "Find E[X] when X is 0 with probability 0.4 and 1 with probability 0.6.",
        stimulus: "weighted average",
        answer: "0.6",
        distractors: ["0.4", "1", "0.5"],
        explanation: "E[X]=0·0.4+1·0.6=0.6.",
      });
    }

    function bernoulliQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "Which distribution models one yes/no trial?",
        stimulus: "success or failure once",
        answer: "Bernoulli",
        distractors: ["Binomial", "Normal", "Uniform"],
        explanation: "A Bernoulli distribution has one trial with two outcomes.",
      });
    }

    function binomialQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "Which distribution counts successes in fixed independent trials?",
        stimulus: "n trials, same p",
        answer: "Binomial",
        distractors: ["Bernoulli", "Normal", "Continuous uniform"],
        explanation: "The binomial distribution counts successes across repeated Bernoulli trials.",
      });
    }

    function uniformQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "A fair die is an example of which discrete pattern?",
        stimulus: "all six faces equally likely",
        answer: "uniform",
        distractors: ["normal", "binomial", "geometric"],
        explanation: "Uniform means all listed outcomes have the same probability.",
      });
    }

    function normalQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "Which distribution is often used for bell-shaped measurement noise?",
        stimulus: "centered around a mean",
        answer: "Normal",
        distractors: ["Bernoulli", "Uniform", "Permutation"],
        explanation: "The normal distribution is a common model for many measurement errors.",
      });
    }

    function weightedAverageQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "Compute the expected value of 0 with probability 0.5 and 10 with probability 0.5.",
        stimulus: "weighted average",
        answer: "5",
        distractors: ["10", "0", "0.5"],
        explanation: "E[X]=0·0.5+10·0.5=5.",
      });
    }

    function possibleOutcomeQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "Can an expected value be impossible as a single outcome?",
        stimulus: "fair die mean is 3.5",
        answer: "yes",
        distractors: ["no", "only for coins", "only if variance is 0"],
        explanation: "Expected value is a long-run average, not necessarily an actual outcome.",
      });
    }

    function varianceQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "What does larger variance usually mean?",
        stimulus: "spread around the mean",
        answer: "outcomes are more spread out",
        distractors: ["mean is always larger", "probabilities do not sum to 1", "outcomes are impossible"],
        explanation: "Variance measures average squared distance from the mean.",
      });
    }

    function fairGameQuestion() {
      return question({
        prompt: "A game costs $5 and has expected payout $5. What is its expected net gain?",
        stimulus: "net gain = payout - cost",
        answer: "$0",
        distractors: ["$5", "-$5", "$10"],
        explanation: "Expected net gain is expected payout minus cost: 5-5=0.",
      });
    }

    function question({ prompt, stimulus, answer, distractors, explanation }) {
      return { prompt, stimulus, answer, options: shuffle([answer, ...distractors]), explanation };
    }

    function radioList(options, onSelect) {
      const name = `probability-checkpoint-${Math.random().toString(36).slice(2)}`;
      const container = element("div", { class: "quiz-options" });
      options.forEach((option) => {
        const input = element("input", { type: "radio", name, value: option });
        input.addEventListener("change", () => onSelect(option));
        container.append(element("label", {}, input, element("span", {}, option)));
      });
      return container;
    }

    function stimulus(text) {
      return element("pre", { class: "quiz-stimulus" }, text);
    }

    function element(tag, attrs = {}, ...children) {
      const node = document.createElement(tag);
      Object.entries(attrs).forEach(([key, value]) => node.setAttribute(key, value));
      node.append(...children);
      return node;
    }

    function choice(values) {
      return values[Math.floor(Math.random() * values.length)];
    }

    function shuffle(values) {
      const copy = values.slice();
      for (let index = copy.length - 1; index > 0; index -= 1) {
        const other = Math.floor(Math.random() * (index + 1));
        [copy[index], copy[other]] = [copy[other], copy[index]];
      }
      return copy;
    }
  })();
</script>
